segments.text: Then Job answered,
Listen diligently to my speech. Let this be your consolation.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Allow me, and I also will speak. After I have spoken, mock on.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As for me, is my complaint to man? Why shouldn’t I be impatient?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look at me, and be astonished. Lay your hand on your mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I remember, I am troubled. Horror takes hold of my flesh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Why do the wicked live, become old, yes, and grow mighty in power?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Their child is established with them in their sight, their offspring before their eyes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Their bulls breed without fail. Their cows calve, and don’t miscarry.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They send out their little ones like a flock. Their children dance.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They sing to the tambourine and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the pipe.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They spend their days in prosperity. In an instant they go down to Sheol.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They tell God, ‘Depart from us, for we don’t want to know about your ways.
segments.speaker: Wicked People

segments.text: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What profit should we have, if we pray to him?’
segments.speaker: Wicked People

segments.text: Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand. The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out, that their calamity comes on them, 
that God distributes sorrows in his anger?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: How often is it that they are as stubble before the wind, as chaff that the storm carries away?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You say, ‘God lays up his iniquity for his children.’Let him recompense it to himself, that he may know it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let his own eyes see his destruction. Let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For what does he care for his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Shall any teach God knowledge, since he judges those who are high?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: One dies in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His pails are full of milk. The marrow of his bones is moistened.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Another dies in bitterness of soul, and never tastes of good.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They lie down alike in the dust. The worm covers them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Behold, I know your thoughts, the plans with which you would wrong me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Haven’t you asked wayfaring men? Don’t you know their evidences,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity, that they are led out to the day of wrath?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who will declare his way to his face? Who will repay him what he has done?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet he will be borne to the grave. Men will keep watch over the tomb.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The clods of the valley will be sweet to him. All men will draw after him, 
as there were innumerable before him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So how can you comfort me with nonsense, because in your answers there remains only falsehood?
segments.speaker: Job
